# hierarchical-classification

Purpose: Hierarchical text classification with pluggable node-level backends.
Python: 3.12+

## Install
Core:
uv add hierarchical-classification

From source:
uv sync

Optional backends:
uv sync --extra sklearn
uv sync --extra huggingface
uv sync --extra llm

## Main API
- src.models.HierarchyNode
- src.classifier.HierarchicalClassifier
- src.models.NodeClassifier protocol

Factory:
HierarchicalClassifier.from_classifier(node_classifier, hierarchy)

Inference:
classifier.classify(utterance: str) -> str  # leaf node name

## NodeClassifier contract
Implement:
predict_proba(utterance: str, node: HierarchyNode) -> np.ndarray[np.float32]

Rules:
- return one score per direct child in node.children order
- prefer normalized probabilities (sum ~= 1)
- child names should be stable and unique per sibling set

## Built-in integrations
Sklearn:
- import: src.integrations.sklearn.SklearnNodeClassifier
- wrapped estimator must expose predict_proba and classes_
- classes_ should include every child label that may be visited

Hugging Face zero-shot:
- import: src.integrations.huggingface.HuggingFaceZeroShotClassifier
- child node names are used as candidate labels

LiteLLM:
- import: src.integrations.llm.LLMNodeClassifier
- set provider credentials (example: OPENAI_API_KEY)

## Minimal usage pattern
1) Build a HierarchyNode tree.
2) Create or select a NodeClassifier.
3) Build classifier with HierarchicalClassifier.from_classifier(...).
4) Call classify(text) and use returned leaf label.
